races.race_name: Here Come The Girls EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Fern Clyde
runners.spotlight: Built upon previous promise to get off the mark at the fourth attempt at Pontefract (6f, good) recently; carries a 4lb penalty against some unexposed rivals today and on breeding there are reservations regarding the step up in trip, but she has strong claims if she stays; wears a first-time hood.
runners.comment: Won 6f maiden at Pontefract on fourth start; strong claims if she stays

runners.name: Little Lady Karen
runners.spotlight: No match for the impressive winner when last of three at 6-1 on this month's debut at Hamilton (6f, good), and she could do with settling better over this longer trip; however, she still showed some promise in that warm little maiden and is open to improvement for last year's winning stable.
runners.comment: Could do with settling better but open to improvement for last year's winning yard

runners.name: Lady Caroline Lamb
runners.spotlight: 11-2 from 9-1 for her debut at Kempton (7f, AW) a fortnight ago, when she kept on well for a promising third; today's extra yardage seems a likely positive and she's a key player.
runners.comment: Backed into 11-2 when promising third on debut over 7f at Kempton; key player

runners.name: Cousin Rachel
runners.spotlight: She has a useful pedigree and is one to watch in the betting on this second start; however, she was 13l behind Fern Clyde when eighth of 11 at 9-1 on her recent debut at Pontefract (6f, good) and needs to be an entirely different proposition this afternoon.
runners.comment: Useful pedigree but well beaten behind Fern Clyde on her recent debut at Pontefract
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

races.race_name: Beverley Racecourse Supporting Racing Staff Week Handicap

runners.name: Roaring Ralph
runners.spotlight: Has been in tremendous form this year, winning five of his last six starts, the most recent under Rhys Elliott at Wetherby (1m, good to firm) eight days ago; unpenalised for that success and holds good claims in hat-trick bid.
runners.comment: Has won 5 of his last 6 starts, including off this mark at Wetherby last week; good claims

runners.name: Sunny Orange
runners.spotlight: Four of his five wins have been at this track (two over C&D); returned to form following a couple of lesser efforts when fourth at Wetherby (1m, good to firm) eight days ago but he was behind Roaring Ralph on that occasion.
runners.comment: Four of his five wins have been at this track; behind Roaring Ralph at Wetherby last time

runners.name: Coolree
runners.spotlight: Four of his five wins have been here over 8.5f (good to soft/good to firm), the most recent last month off 4lb lower, and he was runner-up to This Farh over C&D next time; well held at Chester (7.5f, soft) 11 days ago but it would be no surprise to see him bounce back returned to this track.
runners.comment: Loves it here, with four of his five wins coming at this track over 8.5f; may bounce back

runners.name: Tattie Bogle
runners.spotlight: Doubled his tally when winning at Musselburgh (7f, good) in April off 2lb lower; raced too close to the pace next time but has been placed in each of his last three starts, including at Catterick (7f, good to soft) 12 days ago; chance.
runners.comment: Won at Musselburgh in April off 2lb lower; placed in each of his last three starts; chance

runners.name: This Farh
runners.spotlight: Often front-runner who won at Leicester (7f, good) in April and over this C&D (good to firm) last month; bit below his best at Chepstow 17 days ago but soft ground may have been against him (also lost a shoe).
runners.comment: Often front-runner who won over C&D last month; soft ground a possible excuse last time

runners.name: Midsummer Storm
runners.spotlight: 550,000euros breeze-up 2yo who failed to build on his debut effort when an 11.5l third of 11 in a Newcastle maiden (7f, AW) in March; still looked in need of the experience in a novice at Nottingham (8.5f, good) 45 days ago but may do better now handicapping, albeit this opening mark looks stiff.
runners.comment: Still looked in need of the experience at Nottingham last time; makes his handicap debut

races.race_name: Big Racing Weekender Next 3 And 4 July Restricted Maiden Stakes (For Horses In Bands C And D) (GBB)

runners.name: Regal Desire
runners.spotlight: Sent off 150-1 on her debut at Southwell (1m, AW) last month but she showed plenty, finishing second of 11; looked green on that occasion, hanging left under pressure, and she's open to improvement now switched to turf; contender.
runners.comment: 150-1 but showed plenty on her debut at Southwell, finishing second of 11; contender

runners.name: Three No Trumps
runners.spotlight: 100-1 on his debut at Kempton (1m, AW) in November but shaped with plenty of promise, finishing third of 13; well beaten at Doncaster on his reappearance but it was more encouraging here over 1m2f two months ago, finishing third behind two subsequent winners; drops in trip with claims.
runners.comment: Well held on reappearance but third behind two subsequent winners here over 1m2f last time

runners.name: Cherringham
runners.spotlight: Sister to 1m2f winner Mighty Quiet (RPR 78); looked in need of the experience when fifth of eight on her debut at Lingfield (1m2f, soft; 13-2) 17 days ago and may take a step forward.
runners.comment: Looked in need of the experience on her debut (1m2f, soft) and may take a step forward

runners.name: Tamzan
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden but she's been in the first four on seven of those occasions, including in a handicap at Newmarket (1m, good) 39 days ago; may prove vulnerable back in maiden company but has place claims.
runners.comment: 0-8 but she's been in the first four on seven occasions; has solid place claims

runners.name: Pepsea
runners.spotlight: Promising second at Thirsk (1m, good) on his debut but he hung right on quick ground at Hamilton (1m1f) next time; respectable effort when fourth of 12 at Carlisle (1m1f, good to soft) two weeks ago but the likely quicker ground would be a slight concern.
runners.comment: Hung right on quick ground at Hamilton but has shown promise either side of that effort

runners.name: Alterity
runners.spotlight: Well beaten when fifth on her debut at Newmarket (7f, good to soft) last summer but showed more at Hamilton (1m1f, good to firm) 27 days ago; comes from a nice family and she's open to further progress.
runners.comment: Showed more than she did on her debut when third at Hamilton 27 days ago; may do better

runners.name: Feliz Navidad
runners.spotlight: Gelding by Mondialiste; first foal; dam unraced sister to US 5.5f/6f winner Faithful Ruler, half-sister to 10.5f winner Shamwari, out of 6f winning sister to 1m Group 2 winner Viscount Nelson and half-sister to 2yo Group 1 winners Horatio Nelson (7f) and Van Gogh (1m); market should be informative.
runners.comment: Gelding by Mondialiste; dam unraced sister to US 5.5f/6f winner Faithful Ruler

races.race_name: Beverley Annual Badgeholders Handicap

runners.name: Jedhi Knight
runners.spotlight: Won over 2m at Ffos Las for Jedd O'Keeffe last August (good to soft; awarded race by the stewards); tailed off over hurdles on his debut for this yard but he did run respectably back on the Flat three weeks ago following a wind operation; may build on that first start for three months.
runners.comment: Won over 2m last August; respectable effort on the AW last time after a wind operation

runners.name: Dandy's Angel
runners.spotlight: Six-time course winner over 1m2f on a variety of ground, the most recent coming last summer off a 14lb higher mark; confirmed she's back in form when sixth of ten (beaten 2l) here last week and makes the shortlist returned to this longer trip.
runners.comment: Six-time course winner over 1m2f; beaten 2l when sixth here last week; makes the shortlist

runners.name: Princess Vivi
runners.spotlight: Ten-race maiden but she ran one of her better races when second of seven (beaten 3l) here over 8.5f 27 days ago; doesn't appear to be crying out for this step up in trip but she has claims if seeing it out.
runners.comment: 0-10 but ran one of her better races here over 8.5f last time; not crying out for this far

runners.name: Satyress
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden but she was beaten only 3l when fourth of 11 at Pontefract (1m2f, good) last time on her first start for 272 days; may come on for that and her pedigree suggests this longer trip is worth exploring.
runners.comment: 0-7 but beaten only 3l at Pontefract on her return; this longer trip looks worth exploring

runners.name: Arch Legend
runners.spotlight: Dual winner for Charlie Johnston in 2024; runner-up over this C&D last May on his second start for this yard but he's lost his way since, albeit it was his first start for 301 days when beating only one rival home at Newcastle three weeks ago.
runners.comment: Lost his way since finishing second over C&D last May; needs to take a big step forward
runners.owner: S Thompson And Mrs R Carr

runners.name: Bruce Banner
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden hurdle at Musselburgh in November; did his best work late when runner-up at Redcar (1m6f, good) last month but he's lacked the pace to challenge in two starts since over this trip; visor goes back on for cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Runner-up over 1m6f last month but has lacked the pace to challenge over this trip since

runners.name: Lednikov
runners.spotlight: Six of his seven wins have been on the AW at Southwell, the most recent by a wide margin over 1m6f in March under Elle-May Croot; held in four starts since and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Six of his seven wins have been on the AW at Southwell; held on last four starts

runners.name: Sweet Cicely
runners.spotlight: 21-race maiden and she beat only rival home here over 1m2f in first-time blinkers a week ago; steps back up in trip in a switch of headgear and others have stronger claims.
runners.comment: 0-21 and beat only one rival here over 1m2f last time; others have stronger claims

races.race_name: Humberside Airport Handicap

runners.name: Qitaal
runners.spotlight: All three of his wins have been on ground slower than good but he does act on quick ground; went close at Ripon (1m2f, good) five days ago and steps back up in grade slightly with good claims.
runners.comment: Three-time winner; went close at Ripon (1m2f) five days ago and holds good claims

runners.name: Percy's Daydream
runners.spotlight: Made a winning reappearance at Doncaster (1m2f, good to firm; made virtually all) in April but she's been well beaten in two starts since, including over this trip at Leicester 15 days ago; has a bit to prove.
runners.comment: Disappointing in two starts since her winning reappearance at Doncaster; bit to prove

runners.name: Mao Shang Wong
runners.spotlight: Gained his third success when winning over this C&D last month off 3lb lower but wasn't at his best at York (1m2f, good) 11 days ago; unexposed on good to firm but needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Won over C&D last month but wasn't at his best at York last time; needs to bounce back

runners.name: Helmsley
runners.spotlight: Won a Redcar novice (1m2f, good to soft) in October when trained by Ralph Beckett; yet to match that level for this yard but did run well in a first-time tongue-tie at Chester (1m4f, good to soft) ten days ago, albeit aided by the run of things; unraced on ground quicker than good.
runners.comment: Ran well in a first-time tongue-tie at Chester last time (1m4f); drops back in trip

runners.name: Titian
runners.spotlight: Five wins from 39 starts, the most recent coming at York (1m2f, good) last June off a 6lb higher mark than today's; well beaten in two starts since returning from his second wind operation and now has cheekpieces applied.
runners.comment: 6lb below his last winning mark but well beaten on his last two starts; cheekpieces added

races.race_name: Racing TV Extra On racingtv.com Handicap

runners.name: Resdev Time
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden who has been underwhelming in handicaps; however, her best RPR came when third in a 7.4f novice last September on her sole previous visit to Beverley, and it's possible going beyond 1m for the first time might suit this Time Test filly; not ruled out.
runners.comment: 0-9 but best run came on sole visit here and it's possible the step up in trip will suit

runners.name: Wadacre Geisha
runners.spotlight: 0-5 but this front-runner hasn't been beaten far at Beverley (1m2f, good to firm) in her two handicaps, finishing second of nine then fourth of four; this race possibly won't take much winning; firmly in calculations.
runners.comment: Not beaten far in her two handicaps and this race possibly won't take much winning

runners.name: Sahm Naif
runners.spotlight: Achieved best finish yet when fourth of seven, beaten just over 6l, on recent handicap debut at Leicester (1m2f, good) where he went off 11-2; improvement is necessary today but that can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: 4th of 7 on recent h'cap debut at Leicester; improvement necessary but can't be ruled out

runners.name: Royal Sizzler
runners.spotlight: Well beaten at 80-1 or bigger on all three starts (7f-1m1f); however, she goes handicapping off a modest mark and her dam was useful; not discounted, and the betting could be informative.
runners.comment: Well beaten at 80-1 or bigger on all three starts, but goes handicapping off modest mark

runners.name: Ice Show
runners.spotlight: Well beaten, at increasingly big odds, on her first three starts (7f-1m1f); however, her dam was useful and today's handicap debut provides a first realistic opportunity; up in trip and one to watch in the betting.
runners.comment: Well beaten on first three starts but one to watch in the betting on handicap debut

runners.name: Turton
runners.spotlight: He's achieved little on his three starts (7f-8.3f), but there's potential in his pedigree and he makes his handicap debut off a lowly mark over a new trip and in first-time blinkers; from an in-form stable and he'll be all the more interesting if strong in the betting.
runners.comment: He's achieved little thus far but goes handicapping off a lowly mark for his in-form yard

runners.name: Eva The Deeva
runners.spotlight: Failed to improve when seventh of nine, beaten just over 6l, at 16-1 on last month's handicap debut at Ripon (1m, good); however, she was slowly away and this half-sister to 11.4f-1m6f winner King Victor might improve for the step up in trip; not discounted.
runners.comment: 7th of nine over 1m on handicap debut, when slowly away; might improve for the longer trip

runners.name: King Of Yorkshire
runners.spotlight: Very early days for this half-brother to three winners, who may have needed last month's handicap/seasonal debut at Redcar (1m, good); however, he was tailed off at 14-1 and has to leave that run miles behind.
runners.comment: Half-brother to three winners but tailed off on last month's handicap debut at Redcar

races.race_name: Go Racing In Yorkshire Future Stars Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Jack Rabbit Slims
runners.spotlight: Only fourth in handicaps at Redcar and Carlisle on last two starts and needs something extra today if he's to get off the mark at the 12th attempt; however, he went close in a C&D seller (good to firm) last month and perhaps the return to Beverley will prompt a bigger performance.
runners.comment: 0-11 and needs to better his recent handicap form, but the return to Beverley could help

runners.name: Sparkling Wine
runners.spotlight: Took a step forward when second of six on recent handicap debut at Catterick (5f, good to soft), albeit no match for the odds-on winner; trainer is a dab hand with sprinters and she's open to further improvement.
runners.comment: Took a step forward when second on handicap debut and she's open to further improvement

runners.name: Dabbling
runners.spotlight: She's dropping down the weights but her form has gone the wrong way in handicaps and she's 0-7; needs to turn things around.
runners.comment: She's dropping down the weights but form has gone the wrong way in handicaps and she's 0-7

runners.name: Oasis Cover
runners.spotlight: Promising third on handicap debut at Nottingham (5f, good) last month and bounced back from lesser run at Newcastle (5f, AW) when runner-up at Redcar (6f, good) on Saturday; the return to 5f doesn't seem an obvious plus but the stiff track will help to compensate; firmly in calculations.
runners.comment: Runner-up over 6f at Redcar on Saturday; the stiff track is a positive returned to 5f
runners.owner: All About York Partnership And Partner

runners.name: Tanaka
runners.spotlight: She's raced six times and best effort yet when fifth of eight at 33-1 at Ripon (5f, good) last Wednesday, but another step forward is necessary.
runners.comment: Best effort yet when fifth at Ripon last Wednesday, but another step forward is necessary

runners.name: Brain Freeze
runners.spotlight: Off the mark on her eighth start at Redcar (5f, good to firm) last month and another good run when third at Ripon (5f, good) last Wednesday, despite an early stumble; solid contender.
runners.comment: Won at Redcar then third at Ripon despite an early stumble; solid contender

runners.name: Power Of Chora
runners.spotlight: Has shown hints of promise among her six (winless) starts and she suffered costly interference leaving the stalls at Doncaster recently; things may click at some point but this first attempt at 5f isn't guaranteed to suit and others arrive with more compelling claims.
runners.comment: Has shown hints of promise, but she's 0-6 and others arrive with more compelling claims

races.race_name: South Wales Medical Event Cover Handicap

runners.name: On Edge
runners.spotlight: Consistent and probably a career-best to win after a break at Chepstow (5f, good to firm; fine on softer); only 2lb higher so has to be taken seriously.
runners.comment: Consistent and probably a career-best to win after a break at Chepstow; up 2lb

runners.name: Lohoobb
runners.spotlight: Unraced on ground easier than good; beaten favourite in all his three handicaps but there were encouraging signs, especially with a view to dropping back to sprinting; considered.
runners.comment: Beaten favourite in all his three handicaps but dropping back to 6f could work

runners.name: Proof
runners.spotlight: Heavy-ground winner as 2yo so conditions will be fine and he won on the AW in April; not a lot to shout about since but he was highly tried last time.
runners.comment: Capable sprinter on his day and this is a lot easier than latest Epsom assignment

runners.name: Punchbowl Flyer
runners.spotlight: Infrequent winner these days but always prominent when holding on at Chepstow (6f, good) four days ago; this looks tougher under a 4lb penalty (Alexandra Egan rides again).
runners.comment: Narrow winner four days ago but this looks tougher under a 4lb penalty

runners.name: Grey Horizon
runners.spotlight: Had a busy year but holding his form well and nothing wrong with his narrow defeat on good to soft at Windsor last time; solid claims.
runners.comment: Holding his form well and nothing wrong with his narrow defeat on good to soft at Windsor

runners.name: Safari Dream
runners.spotlight: Multiple winner who made all at Salisbury last month (6f, good) and was ridden differently when never involved at Catterick; had no penalty last time and now 5lb higher, but if returned to positive tactics the ground won't be an excuse.
runners.comment: If returned to positive tactics the ground won't be an excuse

runners.name: Hierarchy
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent on the AW this year, aside from winning in February and without a win on turf since 2023; his recent third at Lingfield (soft) was quite remote.
runners.comment: AW winner in Feb; no turf win since 2023 and well held on soft ground last time

races.race_name: Connolly's Red Mills 'Confined' Handicap (For Horses Which Have Not Won In 2026)

runners.name: Harry's Halo
runners.spotlight: Has shown most of his form over 6f, but he's finished bang there over 5f and 6f in his last two runs and he goes well on good ground and softer; solid.
runners.comment: Bang there over 5f and 6f in his last two runs and he goes well on good ground and softer
runners.owner: Jones, Keane, Kryswicki & Kf Racing Club

runners.name: Isle Of Lismore
runners.spotlight: Broke slowly but finished well when 4l behind on good to soft at Catterick recently; that was his second run after a lengthy break; has won on soft.
runners.comment: Broke slowly but finished well when 4l behind on good to soft at Catterick recently

runners.name: Candy Warhol
runners.spotlight: Front-runner; 0-12 on turf but has placed form and he's a three-time AW winner; last run was disappointing (stays 6f) but had been running well prior to that; a greater concern would be the forecast slow ground.
runners.comment: Better than he showed last time but ground this soft is a cause for concern
runners.owner: Homecroft Wealth, Paul J Dixon & Partners

runners.name: Hint Of Humour
runners.spotlight: Three-time AW winner who is 0-6 on grass after finishing fifth of seven at Newbury (5f); also unraced on slow ground, she has enough to prove.
runners.comment: Three-time AW winner who is 0-6 on grass after finishing fifth of seven at Newbury

runners.name: Arabian Cobra
runners.spotlight: Won two 6f handicaps on good ground last summer when with Hugo Palmer; quiet in two runs for current yard (cheekpieces then a visor) and soft ground might not be what he wants.
runners.comment: Dual 6f winner last year for another yard; quiet in two starts for this one

runners.name: Brazen Idol
runners.spotlight: Missed 2025 and hasn't kicked on from an encouraging return on the AW, last time finishing tailed off on soft.
runners.comment: Missed 2025 and hasn't kicked on from an encouraging return on the AW

races.race_name: Vibe Recruit Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: English Time
runners.spotlight: Cost 260,000gns as a yearling; a closer when fourth at Newbury (7f, good) where he was only 2l off subsequent high-profile winner Extremely Zain and then pushed a previous Juddmonte-owned winner close at Doncaster (good to firm); ground this soft will be a first but he's the one to beat on form.
runners.comment: Ground this soft will be a first but he's the one to beat on form

runners.name: Foxy Night
runners.spotlight: Bred to stay at least 1m; positives to take from both her runs, last time coming from behind to be only 2l behind a promising Ralph Beckett horse at Carlisle (1m, soft); proven on the ground, she's seriously considered.
runners.comment: Ran well behind a promising Beckett horse at Carlisle and ground was soft that day

runners.name: West Byfleet
runners.spotlight: Stepped up on Newbury debut with a promising second at Goodwood (1m, good) and the two he divided have run well since; he was keeping on that day and is back in trip here, albeit on slower ground; every chance if coping with this surface.
runners.comment: Runner-up over 1m at Goodwood and the two he divided have run well since

runners.name: Fallen Logic
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to 5.7f/7f winner North View (RPR 88); dam unraced half-sister to 1m Group 1 winner Fallen For You; useful pedigree but likely best watched on this belated debut.
runners.comment: Useful pedigree but likely best watched on this belated debut

runners.name: Malakai Kite
runners.spotlight: Beaten about 6l in 1m/7f maidens and he'll need more here to threaten a breakthrough.
runners.comment: Beaten about 6l in 1m/7f maidens and he'll need more here to threaten a breakthrough

runners.name: Miss Gold
runners.spotlight: 100-1 when tailed off in this new headgear combinaton at Bath (extended 5f); no appeal.
runners.comment: 100-1 when tailed off in this new headgear combinaton at Bath (extended 5f); no appeal

runners.name: Wid
runners.spotlight: Wootton Bassett filly; ended up well held on last September's debut at Doncaster (7f, good), but didn't appear to be unfancied at 5-1 and she's no doubt capable of better; market informative.
runners.comment: Well held on last September's debut but didn't appear to be unfancied at 5-1

runners.name: Fozzy Osbourne
runners.spotlight: Beaten double-digit lengths in 6f maidens at Salisbury and Newbury; probably one for handicaps.
runners.comment: Beaten double-digit lengths in 6f maidens at Salisbury and Newbury; one for handicaps

runners.name: Prophet Squeeze
runners.spotlight: A lowly rated maiden over jumps who has shown nothing in two Flat novices (1m2f).
runners.comment: A lowly rated maiden over jumps who has shown nothing in two Flat novices (1m2f)

races.race_name: Ffos Las Racecourse Charity Skydive For Ty Hafan Handicap

runners.name: Typeface
runners.spotlight: Easy winner off 5lb lower over C&D last August (good) and runner-up three times since then; however, below par both runs this term and slower ground could be troublesome.
runners.comment: C&D winner; below par both runs this term and slow ground could be troublesome

runners.name: Buckland Belle
runners.spotlight: Turf debut when edging home at Windsor last month (1m, good to firm) and wasn't beaten far from off the pace on her return visit (good to soft); has to be feared off an untouched mark.
runners.comment: Third at Windsor 15 days ago and has to be feared off an untouched mark

runners.name: Sorted
runners.spotlight: He's gone very close in an AW handicap (in March) and should be okay on this slow ground as a son of Mayson; considered.
runners.comment: He's gone very close in an AW handicap (in March) and should be okay on the slow ground

runners.name: Mooretown Lad
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish winner; hold-up performer who finished well for second behind Scarfo off his much-reduced mark at Chepstow (1m, soft); needs a strong pace and he has major claims if he gets one.
runners.comment: Needs a strong pace and he has major claims if he gets one after a recent second

runners.name: Betty Lemon
runners.spotlight: Uncompetitive in five races at two and no better on comeback run when tried in a hood; hard to warm to.
runners.comment: Uncompetitive in five races at two and no better on comeback run when tried in a hood

runners.name: Racing Demon
runners.spotlight: Yet again he missed the break before going down narrowly at Chepstow last week (1m, good; handles softer); too risky when he's almost certain to give himself another uphill battle by starting slowly.
runners.comment: Too risky when almost certain to give himself another uphill battle by starting slowly

runners.name: Uncle Albert
runners.spotlight: Only win came in March 2024 when trained in Ireland; has lost his way this year and finished last on stable debut despite going off favourite.
runners.comment: Lost his way this year and finished last on stable debut despite going off favourite

runners.name: Beau Chevalier
runners.spotlight: Poor so far and was tailed off on his handicap debut (1m, good) when tried in blinkers.
runners.comment: Poor so far and was tailed off on his handicap debut (1m, good) when tried in blinkers

runners.name: Eye Of The Water
runners.spotlight: Having his 105th career start and the last of his eight wins was in August 2023; beaten 4.5l into third at Bath last time and others appeal more.
runners.comment: Having his 105th career start and the last of his eight wins was in August 2023

runners.name: Angel's Call
runners.spotlight: Only win and best form has been at sprint distances and yet to achieve anything of note beyond 6f.
runners.comment: Only win and best form has been at sprint distances

runners.name: Scarfo
runners.spotlight: Had a favourable track position when scoring at Chepstow (1m, soft) but came up shy back there off this 2lb higher mark when ridden by Charlie Tucker; only 2-34 but he should at least give his running.
runners.comment: Only 2-34 but he should at least give his running in current vein of form

races.race_name: Morgan Enviromental Classified Stakes

runners.name: Whiskey Sunrise
runners.spotlight: Can win a race and the ground was soft when beaten a shade under 4l in a 1m handicap at Chepstow most recently (first-time tongue-tie); thereabouts.
runners.comment: Ground was soft when beaten a shade under 4l in a 1m handicap at Chepstow

runners.name: Belle Of Kt
runners.spotlight: Has 3.5l to find with Pureis King on their Lingfield clash but her consistency ticks a big box at this level.
runners.comment: Has 3.5l to find with Pureis King on their Lingfield clash but she's consistent

runners.name: Knightmare
runners.spotlight: Regressive maiden but hasn't been running too badly and it was a classified race in which he was second two starts back (8.6f); should run well in new blinkers.
runners.comment: It was a classified race in which he was second two starts back (8.6f)

runners.name: Pureis King
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner who appreciated the drop in class when doing down by only a neck in one of these at Lingfield (1m2f, good); clear claims if handling the slow ground.
runners.comment: Appreciated the drop in class when doing down by only a neck in one of these at Lingfield

runners.name: Go For Gold
runners.spotlight: Showed precious little last season at 7f/1m; only 13-2 for her handicap debut at Chepstow (1m2f, good to firm) where she was reportedly unsuited by that track's undulations; could well leave that run behind and cheekpieces are tried.
runners.comment: Reportedly unsuited by undulations of Chepstow when 13-2 for her handicap debut

runners.name: A Daughters Love
runners.spotlight: Had eight races but only two for this yard; from 10lb out of the weights she hit the frame in a 1m2f handicap at Bath and definite claims with a repeat of that.
runners.comment: From 10lb out of the weights she hit the frame in a 1m2f handicap at Bath

runners.name: Elora Prince
runners.spotlight: 0-11 in Ireland; ran okay to be midfield in a 1m2f handicap at Bath on yard debut, well enough to consider at this level.
runners.comment: 0-11 in Ireland; ran okay to be midfield in a 1m2f handicap at Bath on yard debut

runners.name: Blue Birdie
runners.spotlight: This is easier than her three novice races (1m-1m3f) but she has a lot to prove nonetheless.
runners.comment: This is easier than her 3 novice races (1m-1m3f) but she has a lot to prove nonetheless

runners.name: Paps Of Jura
runners.spotlight: Fairly uncompetitive in all his nine races, in this headgear combination in last two starts; needs to find something from somewhere and there are doubts about the slow ground.
runners.comment: Fairly uncompetitive in all his nine races, including classified heats

runners.name: Northern Soldier
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in maidens and again in a classified race on the AW 21 days ago; was 50-1 that day in a first-time visor; now goes in blinkers.
runners.comment: Well beaten in maidens and again in a classified race on the AW 21 days ago

races.race_name: Zambezi Magic Handicap

runners.name: Havana Tobouggaloo
runners.spotlight: Her neck defeat on good to soft at Bath (1m) represents useful form for the level; wasn't as good at Yarmouth but possibilities if forgiven that; stays 1m2f.
runners.comment: Below par latest but previous second at Bath is useful form for this level

runners.name: Yokkell
runners.spotlight: On a losing run but finished well to be close up in 1m2f/1m handicaps at Leicester (good to firm); she can cope with good to soft and should run well.
runners.comment: On a losing run but finished well to be close up in 1m2f/1m handicaps at Leicester

runners.name: Zambezi Magic
runners.spotlight: Loves it here and loves the mud, so this dry spell has done him no favours; probably one for another day.
runners.comment: Loves it here and loves the mud, so this dry spell has done him no favours

runners.name: Bizou
runners.spotlight: Won at Windsor (1m2f, good to firm) and followed up from the front at Wolverhampton (9.4f), albeit narrowly; however, ground slower than good is looking an issue.
runners.comment: Two wins in May and still fairly treated, but good to soft has looked an issue before

runners.name: Abando
runners.spotlight: Beaten less than 10l in the German Derby last year; nowhere near that form for Marco Botti or Jamie Osborne but getting back on slower ground could help.
runners.comment: Nowhere near his peak German form but getting back on slower ground may help

runners.name: Fast Steps
runners.spotlight: Losing run is mounting up but on a good mark and placed in his last two races (1m2f); goes on all ground; hard to rule out.
runners.comment: Losing run is mounting up but on a good mark and placed in his last two races

runners.name: Solanna
runners.spotlight: 7-32 on the AW and 2-19 on turf, but he has performed well on grass in his last two starts, latterly over 1m2f at Yarmouth where he didn't get the clearest of runs; respected.
runners.comment: In a good run of form and didn't get the clearest of passages last time at Yarmouth

runners.name: Meet Me In Meraki
runners.spotlight: Effective on good to soft; most recent AW form is not overly inspiring but on a perfectly good mark and can go well when fresh.
runners.comment: On a perfectly good mark and can go well when fresh
runners.owner: Spiers & Hartwell Ltd & Mrs E Evans

races.race_name: BetVictor 100/1 On England Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race) (Div I)

runners.name: Arabica Queen
runners.spotlight: In the frame in both starts last month, the latest when second of nine at Bath (5.7f, firm; 6-4); form of that race not really working out, though, so a bit more will be needed.
runners.comment: In the frame in both starts last month, but latest Bath second not really working out

runners.name: Big Hitter
runners.spotlight: 12-1 when beaten half a length into second of nine (clear of the third) on her Chepstow debut (5f, soft) 17 days ago; likely to face very different conditions here, but still high on list while the extra furlong should suit on breeding.
runners.comment: Beaten half a length on Chepstow debut; different conditions here but 6f should suit

runners.name: Wateera
runners.spotlight: Overcame market weakness when making a successful debut from a previous winner in a six-runner Wetherby novice (5.5f, good to firm) 19 days ago; her Group 2 entry next weekend suggested she is well-regarded and respected despite the penalty.
runners.comment: Beat a previous winner on Wetherby debut; holds a Group 2 entry; respected despite penalty

runners.name: Miss Tango
runners.spotlight: March foal by Showcasing; 70,000gns foal, 40,000gns yearling; half-sister to 6f/8.5f winner Countess Clara (including 2yo/Jersey; RPR 76); dam 7f AW winner (68), closely related to smart Hong Kong 6f-1m winner Rewarding Hero, out of useful 5f 2yo winner; stable can get them ready first time and Oisin Murphy an interesting booking, being 12-48 (25%) for the yard in last five seasons.
runners.comment: Stable can get them ready first time and Oisin Murphy an interesting booking

runners.name: Celestial Mist
runners.spotlight: March foal by Sergei Prokofiev; half-sister to 6f AW winner Dark Sorceress (RPR 59); dam Group-placed 5f-6.5f 2yo winner (99), half-sister to 5f 2yo winner Dark Illusion, is granddaughter of sister to French 1m2f/10.5f Group 3 winner Trumbaka; stable does have winning 2yo newcomers and worth a market check.
runners.comment: Stable does have winning 2yo newcomers and worth a market check

runners.name: Alice Rocks
runners.spotlight: 33-1 when a tailed-off last of five on her Kempton debut (6f, AW) last month.
runners.comment: Tailed-off last of five on her Kempton debut last month
runners.owner: Mps Racing And Jjg Racing Partnership

runners.name: Bossy Angel
runners.spotlight: Sent off 28-1 when beaten a long way into ninth of ten on her Windsor debut (5f, good to firm) this month; stable won this with a twice-raced filly two years ago.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way into ninth of ten on her Windsor debut this month

runners.name: Lushill
runners.spotlight: March foal by Magna Grecia; half-sister to winners Welcome Sight (French 6.5f/9.5f including AW; RPR 82) and Super Mirage (1m 2yo; 73); dam unraced sister to useful 6f winner City Girl, out of 6f Listed winner, is granddaughter of top sprinter Lochsong; stable doesn't have many winning 2yo newcomers.
runners.comment: Stable doesn't have many winning 2yo newcomers

runners.name: Sugar Yes Please
runners.spotlight: February foal by Mohaather; first foal; dam 7f/1m winner (including AW; RPR 69), half-sister to winners Zain Sarinda (Listed-placed 9.4f) and Indian Sounds (5f-7f including 2yo), out of German 1m2f Listed winner; worth a market check on debut for stable that has won this race (or a division of it) three times with newcomers since 2016.
runners.comment: Worth a market check on debut for stable that has won this race three times since 2016

runners.name: Secret Rival
runners.spotlight: Sent off 16-1 and ran green when a well-beaten eighth of nine on her Chepstow debut (5f, soft) 17 days ago; should have come on for the experience, but she has around 13l to find with Big Hitter.
runners.comment: Ran green when a well-beaten eighth of nine on her Chepstow debut 17 days ago

races.race_name: BetVictor 100/1 On England Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race) (Div II)

runners.name: Global Success
runners.spotlight: Both starts at Bath in April, showing ability on her debut (5f, good) but taking a step backwards (5.7f, firm) ten days later; nurseries may provide her with better opportunities.
runners.comment: Both starts at Bath, showing ability on her debut but taking a step backwards next time

runners.name: Chilli
runners.spotlight: Sent off 25-1 and showed signs of greenness on her Chepstow debut (5f, soft) 17 days ago, but she kept on to finish a never-nearer fourth of nine (runner-up Big Hitter runs in the first division); dam's side of pedigree suggests the extra furlong should suit and respected for stable with a fine record in this race.
runners.comment: Kept on to finish a never-nearer fourth on her Chepstow debut; extra furlong should suit

runners.name: Yahaira
runners.spotlight: Ran her best race so far when fourth of nine at Bath (5.7f, firm) four weeks ago, but was still beaten 6l (runner-up Arabica Queen runs in the first division); needs to find more for the cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Ran best race when fourth at Bath four weeks ago, but was still beaten 6l; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Greek Symphony
runners.spotlight: Did best of the newcomers when beaten around 6l into third of eight behind a nice filly on her Goodwood debut (6f, soft; 10-1) 11 days ago; a player if coming on at all from that.
runners.comment: Third on her Goodwood debut 11 days ago; a player if coming on at all from that

runners.name: Sequel Star
runners.spotlight: April foal by Cotai Glory; 36,000euros yearling; resold 50,000euros; dam 5f-1m winner (including 2yo; RPR 96), sister to 1m Group 1 winner Champers Elysees and 6f 2yo winner Elzem, out of maiden half-sister to Italian 1m Group 3 winner She Bat; stable doesn't have many winning 2yo newcomers, but still worth monitoring in the market.
runners.comment: Stable doesn't have many winning 2yo newcomers, but still worth monitoring in the market

runners.name: Sovereign Beach
runners.spotlight: Well held in both starts including when around 4l behind Greek Symphony at Goodwood debut 11 days ago; hood given a go, but she looks more of a nursery type.
runners.comment: Well held in both starts; looks more of a nursery type; hood on

runners.name: Clear Horizon
runners.spotlight: 25-1 when seventh of ten on her Lingfield debut (6f, AW) 19 days ago; needs a big step forward.
runners.comment: Seventh on her Lingfield AW debut 19 days ago; needs a big step forward

runners.name: So Tempting
runners.spotlight: 33-1 when eighth of ten on her Kempton debut (6f, AW) last month; improvement needed, albeit the stable won this with a once-raced filly in 2023.
runners.comment: Eighth on her Kempton AW debut last month; improvement needed

runners.name: Courage Best
runners.spotlight: 50-1 when a well-held seventh of 11 on her Windsor debut (6f, good to soft) 15 days ago; much more needed; stable won this race two years ago.
runners.comment: Well-held seventh on her Windsor debut 15 days ago; much more needed

runners.name: Havananother
runners.spotlight: 12-1 when a well-beaten fifth of six on her Chepstow debut (6f, good to soft) eight days ago; improvement required.
runners.comment: Well-beaten fifth of six on her Chepstow debut eight days ago; improvement required

races.race_name: Darley EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Bayside View
runners.spotlight: 11-1 and led when neck second of six on recent debut at Doncaster (6.5f, good to firm), coming clear with odds-on newcomer; that was a promising start and she's bred to be suited by today's extra yardage; strong claims.
runners.comment: Neck second of six on recent debut at Doncaster and she has strong claims

runners.name: Anad
runners.spotlight: 150,000euros yearling who has a useful pedigree and was favourite when winning on last month's debut at Wolverhampton (7f, AW); did it by a head and improvement is probably needed if she's to defy a penalty today, but she defied her evident inexperience last time and is a likeable prospect.
runners.comment: Favourite when winning on debut in May; did it narrowly but she's a likeable prospect

runners.name: Roosike
runners.spotlight: 20,000euros foal, £60,000 yearling; fourth foal; Sioux Nation half-sister to winners So Chic (7f/1m AW; RPR 69) and Diego Spirit (Italian 6f 2yo/1m); dam maiden from useful family; it's possible she'll need this debut outing but a market move would be interesting.
runners.comment: £60,000 yearling; half-sister to two winners; may need debut run but check the betting

runners.name: Approved
runners.spotlight: By Kingman; first foal; dam 1m-2m winner (including 2yo/Listed), half-sister to two winners out of useful 7f 2yo/1m4f winner; likely to improve for this debut run but no surprise if she goes well; watch the betting.
runners.comment: First foal; dam 1m-2m winner (including 2yo/Listed); no surprise if she goes well on debut

runners.name: Goblet
runners.spotlight: There's potential in her pedigree but she was tailed off at 80-1 on debut here (6.5f, good to firm) this month and may be best watched for now.
runners.comment: There's potential in her pedigree but she was tailed off at 80-1 on debut here
runners.owner: J F Dean, Mrs J Scott & Lady Trenchard

races.race_name: Venture Security Handicap

runners.name: Creative Queen
runners.spotlight: Responded well to the fitting of blinkers when beating three rivals in a Yarmouth novice (6f, soft) last month; has another furlong and probably different ground to contend with, but that latest form has since been franked by the runner-up; high on list on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Form of last month's novice win has been franked; high on list on handicap debut

runners.name: Starlight Sami
runners.spotlight: Wasn't winning out of turn when scoring by a neck from 13 rivals at Salisbury (7f, good) last month; second and seventh horses have won since so shortlisted from 3lb higher.
runners.comment: Beat 13 rivals at Salisbury last month; form has been franked; shortlisted from 3lb higher

runners.name: Seeing Stars
runners.spotlight: Record in first five starts on the AW read 22213, but she showed she could handle turf when beaten 2l into fourth of ten at Wetherby (7f, good to firm) 19 days ago; each-way claims down 1lb.
runners.comment: Showed she could handle turf when fourth at Wetherby last time; each-way claims

runners.name: White Ladder
runners.spotlight: 0-8 though placed in four of his last five starts; needs a bit more to get his head in front and another frame finish may be his best hope.
runners.comment: 0-8 though placed in four of his last five starts; needs more to get his head in front

runners.name: Signcastle City
runners.spotlight: Holds his form well despite a busy schedule and appeared to appreciate the drop in trip when beaten half a length into second of 22 at York (7f, good) last time; up 2lb but this is a slightly weaker race; respected.
runners.comment: Second of 22 at York last time; up 2lb but this is a slightly weaker race; respected

runners.name: Lahan Kingman
runners.spotlight: Promising when third of ten behind Touleen on her debut over C&D (good to firm) last August and has finished runner-up in two small-field contests over 6f/7f on the AW since returning in March; return to these conditions may help on handicap debut with blinkers given a go; interesting.
runners.comment: Placed in three starts; return to these conditions may help on handicap debut; blinkers on

runners.name: Sedgemoor
runners.spotlight: Two wins at Newcastle (6f, AW) this year, but not very consistent and was well beaten at Doncaster (7f, good to soft) last month; 2lb out of the weights; stable also runs White Ladder.
runners.comment: Not very consistent and was well beaten at Doncaster last month; 2lb out of the weights

runners.name: Aigeas
runners.spotlight: Made a winning debut in a newcomers' race at Deauville (6.5f, AW) last October, but well held in three starts on turf in the meantime; needs to respond positively to the change of headgear.
runners.comment: Winning debut at Deauville last October but little in three starts on turf; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Trinculo
runners.spotlight: Encouraging return when third of ten in a Kempton maiden (6f, AW) last month and although only third of four at Windsor (6f, good to firm) three weeks later, the winner and fourth have won since; extra furlong should suit and worth a second look on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Third in both starts since returning; worth a second look on handicap debut

runners.name: Bami
runners.spotlight: Ran well in all four starts late last year, including landing a big-field Lingfield novice (7f, AW); will need to hit the ground running in order to be competitive here after 195 days off, though.
runners.comment: Ran well in all four starts late last year, but needs to be ready to go after 195 days off

runners.name: Dakota Breeze
runners.spotlight: Showed some ability in three starts on the AW during the winter and the return to this trip may help, but improvement will be needed on turf/handicap debut after four months off; has been gelded since last seen.
runners.comment: Improvement needed on turf/handicap debut after four months off; gelded since last seen

runners.name: Nakaaha
runners.spotlight: Course winner who is likely to have her ideal ground conditions, but still 3lb above her last winning mark and another furlong may have been preferable.
runners.comment: Still 3lb above her last winning mark and another furlong may have been preferable

runners.name: Documenting
runners.spotlight: Admirable teenager who has a better strike-rate on the AW, but he has won over this C&D; finished a long way behind Signcastle City at York (7f, good) last month and probably best watched.
runners.comment: A long way behind Signcastle City at York last month; probably best watched
runners.owner: Kevin Frost Racing Club & Mike Humphries

runners.name: Noodle Mission
runners.spotlight: Rated 7lb lower than on the AW, but is hard to warm to on his latest efforts; needs the first-time blinkers to make a difference.
runners.comment: Rated 7lb lower than on the AW, but hard to warm to on latest efforts; blinkers on

runners.name: Havana Halo
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in five starts over this trip last year; has shown ability, but will do well to make a winning return in such a competitive contest after 259 days off.
runners.comment: Unplaced in five starts last year; will do well to make a winning return from 259 days off

runners.name: Ararat
runners.spotlight: 7lb below his last winning mark and didn't run badly when fourth of seven on his third start for the yard at Salisbury (7f, good) nine days ago, but he gave plenty of trouble beforehand; others preferred.
runners.comment: Fourth at Salisbury nine days ago, but he gave plenty of trouble beforehand

races.race_name: Jebel Ali Racecourse Handicap

runners.name: Arjack
runners.spotlight: He's raced five times and is yet to finish better than fifth, but he's shown promise and wasn't beaten far in a handicap at Lingfield (7f, AW) in March on his sole run this year; hinted last time that the drop to 6f may suit; no surprise if he gives a good account.
runners.comment: Yet to finish better than 5th but has shown promise; no surprise if he gives good account

runners.name: Albert Cee
runners.spotlight: Two turf wins early in career and he's won twice on AW this year (both 6f), the latest last month; well beaten at Yarmouth a fortnight ago but the soft ground may not have suited; he's not discounted.
runners.comment: Won at Southwell (6f, AW) last month and soft ground may not have suited at Yarmouth since

runners.name: Obsidian Dream
runners.spotlight: Turf maiden winner who showed spark as recently as January (AW) and he's dropped down the weights; however, he's struggled on grass on his last two starts and a major turnaround is needed in first-time blinkers.
runners.comment: He's dropped down the weights but has struggled on grass on his last two starts

runners.name: Twirler
runners.spotlight: Overcame trouble in running to win at Windsor (6f, good to firm) last month and another good run when second under James Glennon at Goodwood (6f, good to soft) since; solid claims.
runners.comment: Won at Windsor last month and good second at Goodwood since; solid claims

runners.name: Forever My Prince
runners.spotlight: Two turf wins, the latest a claimer at Leicester last October on final run for Eve Johnson Houghton; dropping down the weights but hasn't been shaping of late as though he's about to take advantage.
runners.comment: Dropping down the weights but not shaping of late as though he's about to take advantage

runners.name: Escape Plan
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 3yo who won at Kempton (6f, AW) in March on his second handicap start; needs to prove he's just as good on turf but he was badly hampered when making headway at Bath (5.7f, good to firm) last month, when he was sent off favourite; possible player.
runners.comment: 3yo; 6f AW win in March; needs to prove he's just as good on turf but had an excuse latest

runners.name: Exhibitioning
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in Windsor novice (6f, good to firm) last August on her second 2yo start and she's of interest on that form; three lesser runs have followed but her two handicap starts have been on good to soft ground, which may not have been ideal; might leave recent comeback run miles behind.
runners.comment: Well beaten in both h'caps but on good to soft; promise in fast-ground novice last August

runners.name: Yes Waliim
runners.spotlight: Hasn't made an impact this year in his first three handicaps but he made late headway when 4l sixth of eight at Lingfield (5f, good) two starts ago, suggesting a return to 6f would be a good fit, and soft ground may have been against him at Salisbury (6f) subsequently; remains of interest.
runners.comment: Soft ground may not have suited last time; caught the eye keeping on over 5f previously

runners.name: Rage Of Thunder
runners.spotlight: Returned to form when keeping on for fifth at Sandown (5f, good) recently and he's on a handy mark on last year's best 6f form, including a near-miss over C&D (good to firm); firmly in calculations back up in trip.
runners.comment: Back to form when keeping on over 5f at Sandown; firmly in calculations back up in trip

runners.name: The Caribbean
runners.spotlight: Won at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) last December and was third there (7f) next time; absent since heavy defeat at Southwell (7f, AW) in February but he ran well last May following a break and should be fine back on turf and back down to 6f; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Heavy defeat in February last time but in good form previously; not ruled out on return

runners.name: Laughterintherain
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in Lingfield novice (7f, AW) last December on third start; soundly beaten in 1m handicaps on her two starts this season; showed pace last time and it's possible this lightly raced 3yo will be suited by today's first crack at sprinting, but others have more compelling claims.
runners.comment: Today's first crack at sprinting is worth a go but others have more compelling claims

runners.name: Massimo Blue
runners.spotlight: He's on a competitive mark on last October's good AW form but he went off the boil subsequently and this is his first start since January; unproven on turf; needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: He's on a competitive mark on last October's good AW form but he went off the boil after

runners.name: Roman Spring
runners.spotlight: Three wins last year and he's below his last winning mark; reappeared with fairly encouraging third at Salisbury (6f, good to firm) last month but he's failed to shine on his three outings since.
runners.comment: Reappeared with encouraging third at Salisbury last month but he's failed to shine since

runners.name: Monsieur Patat
runners.spotlight: Reappeared with a promising third at Kempton (7f, AW) last month and he's 2lb lower than when winning at Windsor (6f, good) last August; he has a good record at Windsor but can also be effective elsewhere; chance.
runners.comment: Reappeared with promising third last month and he's below his last winning mark; chance

races.race_name: Pump Technology Performance Under Pressure Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Kitty Foyle
runners.spotlight: Usually seen over hurdles these days, but she twice finished runner-up over C&D (good to firm) last summer including when beaten a head by Almuhit in this race; meets him on the same terms so has to be respected.
runners.comment: Beaten a head by Almuhit in this race last year and meets him on the same terms

runners.name: Almuhit
runners.spotlight: Comes into this off the back of winning a three-runner race over hurdles at Fontwell, but he is equally adept on the Flat with two wins in four-runner contests over C&D (good to firm) last summer, including this race off 1lb lower; respected.
runners.comment: Two wins over C&D last summer included this race off 1lb lower; respected

runners.name: Baileys Khelstar
runners.spotlight: Has been a bit up and down since returning from a mammoth absence in April, but his second of five at Catterick (1m6f, good to soft) 12 days ago was handsomly franked by the winner at Newmarket on Saturday; has won over this far and a major player.
runners.comment: Latest second at Catterick franked by the winner at Newmarket on Saturday; major player
runners.owner: Baileys Horse Feeds (F H Nash Ltd)

runners.name: Russian Rumour
runners.spotlight: Usually consistent, but her modest effort at Goodwood (2m, soft) last time was disappointing given she does handle any ground; needs to bounce back from that.
runners.comment: Usually consistent, but modest effort at Goodwood was disappointing; needs to bounce back

runners.name: Taritino
runners.spotlight: Won twice over hurdles in the spring and returned to winning form on the Flat with a convincing success at Salisbury (1m4f, good) nine days ago; 5lb penalty probably not an issue, but he didn't get home in one previous go over this far on the Flat, which leaves him with a bit to prove.
runners.comment: Won at Salisbury last time, but didn't get home in one go over this far on the Flat

races.race_name: Racehorse Ownership From £25 At racingclub.com Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Redbud Sixteen
runners.spotlight: Bolted up under a positive ride to make it sixth-time lucky at Salisbury (1m2f, good) nine days ago; likely to go up a good deal more than her 5lb penalty, but whether her rivals will allow her as much rope this time is uncertain.
runners.comment: Made all and bolted up at Salisbury last time, but not sure this will pan out the same way

runners.name: Harlington
runners.spotlight: Has won on turf, but has a much better strike-rate on the AW including a hat-trick earlier this year; only 1lb lower than when third of four at Yarmouth (1m2f, good to firm) last month and may need more help.
runners.comment: Has a much better strike-rate on the AW; may need more help from the handicapper

runners.name: Raintown
runners.spotlight: 8lb lower than when winning easily here (1m4f, good to firm) last July, but hasn't matched that form since and was a long last of four at Bath (11.7f, firm) six weeks ago; can be slowly away.
runners.comment: Won easily off 8lb higher here last July, but hasn't matched that form since
runners.owner: Ms Caroline Instone & J S Moore

runners.name: Shady Bay
runners.spotlight: Course winner who made a successful reappearance at Nottingham (1m2f, good) in April and again ran well to finish third of seven in a 0-90 back there last month (fourth and fifth won next time); up another 1lb, but still of serious interest back at this level and has won over this trip on the AW.
runners.comment: Form of latest third in a 0-90 at Nottingham has been franked; of serious interest
runners.owner: P Afia, E Hobson, A Pegley, C&r Russell

runners.name: Brodie's Boy
runners.spotlight: 0-20 in handicaps and was behind Grey Fox when last of eight at Windsor (1m2f, good to firm) this month; down the pecking order.
runners.comment: 0-20 in handicaps and was behind Grey Fox last time; down the pecking order

runners.name: Grey Fox
runners.spotlight: Course winner who has dropped 2lb lower then last successful and has twice won off the back of an absence; however, he didn't show enough back from 313 days off at Windsor (1m2f, good to firm) this month to be of immediate interest.
runners.comment: Didn't show enough on his reappearance this month to be of immediate interest

runners.name: Robusto
runners.spotlight: Won a four-runner race at Salisbury (1m4f, good to firm) as a 3yo, but all five wins since have come on the AW; rated 9lb lower on grass but still hard to warm to on a rare turf appearance.
runners.comment: Last five wins have come on the AW; hard to warm to on a rare turf appearance

runners.name: Expressionless
runners.spotlight: In and out since winning at Leicester (1m4f, good) in April and was disappointing at Goodwood (1m6f, soft) 11 days ago given he has won over that far and in testing ground; has plenty of form on good to firm, but has a bit to prove; often misses the break.
runners.comment: In and out since winning at Leicester in April; disappointing last time; bit to prove

runners.name: Tiger
runners.spotlight: Ex-French gelding who ran his best race for this yard when beaten half a length at Doncaster (1m4f, good to soft) nine days ago; races off the same mark and dropping a furlong no bad thing, but he is unraced on ground faster than good.
runners.comment: Just beaten at Doncaster last time, but is unraced on ground faster than good

runners.name: Vino Sauro
runners.spotlight: 0-6; hasn't built on early promise, including three poor efforts on turf/dirt in Meydan, and little better in a Wolverhampton maiden (8.6f, AW) this month; only of interest if backed.
runners.comment: 0-6 and hasn't built on early promise; only of interest if backed

runners.name: Yokohama
runners.spotlight: Was winning for the first time since his debut when just prevailing over 1m4f here (good) last month, but may not have been suited by the conditions when third of six at Yarmouth (11.5f, soft) 12 days ago; each-way claims.
runners.comment: Won here last month, but may have found the ground too soft last time; each-way claims

races.race_name: Proper Job Amateur Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Linden Lane
runners.spotlight: 0-13 over hurdles but bounced back from a poor run when close third of six over C&D (good) 11 days ago and remains on a good mark; no surprise if he's on the premises again.
runners.comment: 0-13 over hurdles but bounced back from a poor run when close third over C&D 11 days ago

runners.name: Ugo Bingo
runners.spotlight: Beaten under 10l when seventh in recent C&D (good) contest won by Cogital and continues to slip down the weights, but has not been firing on all cylinders in recent months and needs to up his game.
runners.comment: Not beaten far when seventh over C&D three weeks ago but needs to up his game

runners.name: Saint Polo
runners.spotlight: Well beaten on last month's chasing debut but quickly got back in the groove on return to hurdling last week, winning readily by 7l at Stratford (2m2f, good; second run in cheekpieces); avoids a penalty and has obvious claims.
runners.comment: Avoids penalty for last week's 7l win at Stratford; obvious claims

runners.name: Chillhi
runners.spotlight: Out of form over a range of trips for new stable this spring (five runs; including one on the Flat); drops back to 2m after 42l defeat over 2m7f in refitted cheekpieces (retained) ten days ago; revival needed.
runners.comment: Out of form over a range of trips for new stable this spring; needs to turn a corner

runners.name: Norman Fletcher
runners.spotlight: Useful in his prime but was largely disappointing last season, and suffered 46l defeat at Warwick (2m5f, good) on latest outing five weeks ago; drops in grade again and may benefit from the drop back in trip but needs to turn a corner.
runners.comment: Useful in his prime but has lost his way; drops in trip/grade; revival needed

runners.name: Cogital
runners.spotlight: Held on well to exploit reduced mark over C&D (good; cheekpieces refitted) three weeks ago and can remain competitive after recent 4lb rise, but he's not the force of old and has become inconsistent.
runners.comment: C&D winner off reduced mark this month but this veteran has become inconsistent

runners.name: Roadshow
runners.spotlight: Off since below-par run in October but the form of his breakthrough win at Sedgefield (2m1f, good) in September worked out quite well; cheekpieces (worn once last summer) are given another whirl; contender if fully tuned up.
runners.comment: Off since below-par run in October but September's winning form worked out quite well

runners.name: Applejack Poet
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish 9yo with modest 1-28 strike-rate under both codes; absent since safely held in a chase in October; considered only if lively in the betting before this stable debut.
runners.comment: Has modest strike-rate in Ireland; off since safely held in chase in October; stable debut

races.race_name: Celebrate Racing Staff Week Juvenile Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Sudbury Hill
runners.spotlight: Fairly useful on the Flat at his best; unable to justify odds-on favouritism when second of six on recent hurdling debut at Hexham (2m, good; winner beaten since) but ought to have learned from that experience and might be better suited by this easier track.
runners.comment: Fairly useful at best on the Flat; fair second on hurdle debut at Hexham

runners.name: Affection
runners.spotlight: Won four times on the Flat when based in France and was respectable third of seven in Class 4 handicap on recent British debut (1m2f, soft; RPR 79); this will be the quickest turf she has encountered but she still commands respect on her hurdling debut.
runners.comment: Won four times on the Flat in France; commands respect on hurdle debut

runners.name: Hearts To Heaven
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; dam unraced sister to useful bumper/2m4f hurdle winner Tikkanbar; makes less appealing than the other newcomer on paper.
runners.comment: Fifth foal from sister to useful bumper/2m4f hurdle winner Tikkanbar; makes debut

runners.name: Keep Under Wraps
runners.spotlight: Sixth foal; half-brother to five Flat winners; dam placed 6f 2yo, sister to 2m Group 2 winner Illustrious Blue; has travelled from Cheshire to make his debut (stable's only runner on the card); market helpful.
runners.comment: Out of a sister to a 2m Group 2 Flat winner; has travelled from Cheshire for this

races.race_name: Happy Birthday June O'Dwyer Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Coumeenoole
runners.spotlight: Initially disappointing for new stable this year but easily landed gamble in Bangor handicap (2m7f, good) in March, and did not need to be at same level to justify skinny odds in Uttoxeter novice (2m7f, good) this month; has a big penalty but should still be hard to beat.
runners.comment: Two wins this spring (handicap/novice); should be hard to beat on these terms

runners.name: Outmaster
runners.spotlight: Easily won four-runner maiden point on debut in April then brushed aside modest opposition in C&D maiden hurdle (good) on last month's rules debut, winning by 30l; meets a much tougher rival in Coumeenoole this time but it's very hard to gauge the limit of his ability; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Easily brushed aside modest opposition in C&D maiden hurdle on last month's rules debut

runners.name: Bacardi Blue
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten when 200-1 for run-of-the-mill Worcester maiden (2m, good) on recent hurdle debut; moves up in trip.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten when 200-1 for run-of-the-mill Worcester maiden on recent hurdle debut
runners.owner: D Summersby J May W Dodd

runners.name: Glenary
runners.spotlight: Completed for first time in five-race career when second of two finishers in three-runner maiden point last month but still can't be recommended on this hurdling debut.
runners.comment: Second of two finishers in three-runner maiden point in May; best watched on hurdle debut

runners.name: Keep Off The Sugar
runners.spotlight: Struggled when a 200-1 outsider for first two hurdle runs, the latter over C&D three weeks ago.
runners.comment: Struggled when a 200-1 outsider for first two hurdle runs, the latter over C&D

runners.name: Ballinoulart
runners.spotlight: 0-22 in points and 0-17 under rules; placed a few times off lowly marks in handicap chases in 2023/24 and also in points this year but another minor role is probably his best hope.
runners.comment: Second in two points this year but overall strike-rate is 0-39

races.race_name: EBCS Hideout Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Saucats
runners.spotlight: Seemingly aided by first-time cheekpieces when close second to Makinbecon over C&D (good) last month, but managed only fifth at Uttoxeter (2m4f, good) recently and is now 0-23 over hurdles.
runners.comment: Went close over C&D last month but shade below that form since and now 0-23 over hurdles

runners.name: Katana
runners.spotlight: 0-12 over hurdles but returned to form with creditable second of ten at Worcester (2m, good) this month, after a break; remains on a good mark; major player if in similar mood again.
runners.comment: Returned to form with creditable second at Worcester this month, after a break; player

runners.name: Man Oh Man
runners.spotlight: Went close at Exeter (2m7f, good) in April, but has very mixed record for current stable since the autumn; well beaten over 2m4f on latest outing and now 0-14 over hurdles; not the percentage call.
runners.comment: Went close in April but has 0-14 strike-rate over hurdles and is inconsistent

runners.name: Makinbecon
runners.spotlight: Battled on well to beat Saucats and open account over C&D (good) last month, his sixth start over hurdles; probably needs to improve again to overcome 5lb rise but that's possible.
runners.comment: Battled on well to open account over C&D last month; needs to progress again

runners.name: Warfleet
runners.spotlight: Made inauspicious start to hurdling career, but kept on well for third here (2m1f, good) on handicap debut three weeks ago and today's move back up in trip looks the right move.
runners.comment: Kept on well for third over 2m1f on handicap debut; back up in trip

runners.name: Privilege
runners.spotlight: Runner-up on consecutive C&D starts last year (latterly in this race) but absent since disappointing Flat run last August and returns with hurdle strike-rate standing at 0-17.
runners.comment: Second in this a year ago but absent since disappointing Flat run last August

runners.name: Marhaba The Champ
runners.spotlight: Refused to race on the Flat last month and again looked unenthusiastic on recent handicap hurdle debut here (2m1f, good), albeit he eventually made headway into sixth (behind Warfleet); remains a risky option.
runners.comment: Looked unenthusiastic on handicap hurdle debut, albeit eventually made some headway

runners.name: Chartwell Jock
runners.spotlight: 0-5 over hurdles for Seamus Mullins but made the frame in two good-ground handicaps for him this spring (2m/2m1f); has refitted cheekpieces and Sean Bowen enlisted for this stable debut (same owner); interesting.
runners.comment: Made the frame in both handicaps for Seamus Mullins; Sean Bowen booked on stable debut

runners.name: Phantom Gold
runners.spotlight: Did not get competitive in his three qualifying races, but caught the eye when flashing home from a long way off the pace to take second on handicap debut at Southwell (2m, good) a fortnight ago; back up in trip here and could still have a lot more to offer.
runners.comment: Flashed home to take second on recent handicap debut; still has more to offer

runners.name: Bowling Shane
runners.spotlight: Kept on for third on handicap hurdle debut at Ffos Las (2m, good) in early May, but was below that form when upped to 2m4f this month; now has tongue-tie added; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Placed on last month's handicap debut (2m) but well held since (2m4f); others preferred

runners.name: Crab Road
runners.spotlight: Unplaced all five hurdling starts in Ireland last season and pulled up in handicap here (2m1f, good) on recent stable debut; difficult to enthuse over.
runners.comment: Unplaced all five hurdling starts in Ireland and pulled up here on recent stable debut

runners.name: Wise Counsellor
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in three starts for new stable in early part of this year and also struggled when back on the Flat last month; makes handicap hurdle debut off a lowly mark and with cheekpieces refitted, but has quite a lot to prove.
runners.comment: Makes handicap hurdle debut from a lowly mark but has quite a lot to prove

races.race_name: St. Austell Brewery Handicap Chase

runners.name: Limerick Leader
runners.spotlight: Won this race for Alison Thorpe a year ago (good ground; visored) and returned from layoff with close second for new stable at Southwell (3m, good; first-time blinkers) a fortnight ago; strong claims.
runners.comment: Won this in 2025 and went close on recent stable debut; strong claims

runners.name: Jack's A Legend
runners.spotlight: Needs to bounce back from last month's disappointing run at Cartmel, but was only beaten just over 3l when third over C&D (good to soft) on stable debut in April, despite clouting the penultimate fence; has possibilities if handling today's faster ground.
runners.comment: Below form last month but has claims if judged on C&D third on stable debut in April

runners.name: Walkin Out
runners.spotlight: Won two three-runner points this spring, either side of close third in four-runner Warwick hunter chase (3m, good); has more on her plate on this handicap return/stable debut (same owner), but is well treated on some of last year's form and Sean Bowen is booked.
runners.comment: Won two small-field points this spring; back in a handicap chase with Sean Bowen booked

runners.name: Danny's Friend
runners.spotlight: Came good off lowly mark at Hereford (2m, good) in November and could plausibly have untapped potential over this longer trip, but made low-key reappearance when well-held fourth back at Hereford last month and others in this field have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Won at Hereford (2m) in November but made low-key reappearance there last month

runners.name: Vengeance
runners.spotlight: 0-16 overall but ended last season with three respectable hurdle runs (2m4f-2m7f, good) and faced fairly tough task from out of the weights when last of three over C&D on chasing debut three weeks ago; could have a say.
runners.comment: 0-16 but ended last season in fair form over hurdles and had tough task on chase debut

runners.name: Demoiselle Kap
runners.spotlight: Rejoined Sophie Leech after remote second in selling hurdle last month; made no impact when 50-1 for 2m1f handicap hurdle three weeks later and reverts to chasing with quite a bit to prove; cheekpieces removed.
runners.comment: Well beaten over hurdles last month, having rejoined this stable; now back over fences

runners.name: School For Scandal
runners.spotlight: Five-time chase winner, most recently at Windsor (3m, good to soft) in the autumn; back on a good mark now (even from 1lb out of the weights) and will be fine on the faster ground here, but was pulled up on two of his last three outings (bled from the nose last time) and has become too inconsistent to rely upon.
runners.comment: Five chase wins; back on good mark now but too inconsistent to rely upon
runners.owner: K Johns & Joe Tickle Racing Club

races.race_name: Murray Group Gallop Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Striking A Pose
runners.spotlight: Made all to exploit much-reduced mark over C&D in April and is only 3lb higher today but he's unreliable nowadays and was last of five at Plumpton (3m1f, good) on latest outing four weeks ago.
runners.comment: Made all off reduced mark over C&D in April but is unreliable nowadays
runners.owner: S Clare G Kennington & C Knowles

runners.name: Belgarum
runners.spotlight: Showed the benefit of a break and his latest wind op when making all at Fontwell (2m3f, good) last month; again ran well when close second of three over C&D (good) a fortnight later; respected.
runners.comment: Won over 2m3f last month, after a break, and went close over C&D a fortnight later; player

runners.name: Jafimgoso
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner; took a while to get his act together over hurdles but showed good stamina to lead close home at Ludlow (2m5f, good) last month and was only beaten 1l when upped to 3m at Southwell (good) three weeks ago; ought to be in the thick of things again.
runners.comment: Led close home over 2m5f last month and has since gone close over 3m; good chance

runners.name: Jager Time
runners.spotlight: Came good in 16-runner handicap at Huntingdon (3m1f, good) in November, his eighth attempt, and returned from break with two good efforts this spring, latterly when third (just behind Jafimgoso) at Southwell (3m, good) three weeks ago; another to consider.
runners.comment: Won 16-runner handicap in the autumn and returned with two good runs this spring

runners.name: Cinnodin
runners.spotlight: Six-time Flat winner; opened account over hurdles at Uttoxeter (2m7f, good; refitted cheekpieces) last month, after a break, and ran at least as well when close/clear second over that C&D three weeks later; can make presence felt again over this longer trip.
runners.comment: Won over 2m7f at Uttoxeter in May and ran at least as well when second there last time

runners.name: I Am Spider Man
runners.spotlight: Tailed off on stable debut in April but placed three consecutive times since, most recently when rallying third of ten at Cartmel (3m1f, good) four weeks ago; probably won't be far away.
runners.comment: Tailed off on stable debut but placed three consecutive times since; in the mix again

runners.name: Final Entry
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced since winning 2m juvenile hurdle here in summer 2024; kept on for very respectable third of five over C&D (good) 11 days ago, and remains unexposed over this trip, but today's race is more competitive; swaps cheekpieces for first-time visor.
runners.comment: Ran well when third of five over C&D this month but this race is more competitive

runners.name: Balkardy
runners.spotlight: Multiple hurdle/chase winner; unseated rider in early stages of a chase last month, after a layoff, and was tailed-off 66-1 shot when back over hurdles at Worcester (2m7f, good) 17 days ago; others have much less to prove.
runners.comment: Tailed off when 66-1 at Worcester this month; others have much less to prove

races.race_name: Thatchers Handicap Chase

runners.name: Illogical Logic
runners.spotlight: Ended last season with three poor runs but is now 3lb lower than when making all over C&D (good) in cheekpieces just over a year ago and might be boosted by the returning headgear.
runners.comment: C&D winner last spring; ended last season in poor form but has cheekpieces refitted today

runners.name: Hatos
runners.spotlight: Not yet proven over this far but made the frame on final four outings last season (2m3f-2m4f, good) and was in front before penultimate-fence unseat at Fontwell (3m2f, good) last month; must be considered.
runners.comment: Consistent towards end of last season and was in front when unseating last time; respected

runners.name: Testflight
runners.spotlight: Has modest 1-29 strike-rate under rules but the win was gained over C&D (last summer) and last month's reappearance run was much more encouraging than his eventual 78l defeat suggests; each-way claims at least.
runners.comment: Has modest strike-rate but his was came over C&D; considered each-way

runners.name: Edgewell
runners.spotlight: Suffered dip in form after pair of 3m1f Hereford wins towards end of last season but seemed to run well when third of four at Fontwell (3m2f, good; headgear switched) a fortnight ago and is now 2lb below his last winning mark; enters calculations.
runners.comment: Seemingly back in good nick when third of four at Fontwell recently; enters calculations

runners.name: Unspeakable
runners.spotlight: Returned from break with 36l defeat in April but last month's Fontwell fourth (3m2f, good) was more encouraging and the cheekpieces (worn for the second of last year's two chase wins) are now back on; won't mind if the ground is quicker than good; can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Didn't run badly last time and the cheekpieces (worn for latest win) are back on today

runners.name: Jetty May
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage mare who won over hurdles here (2m5f, good) in April; managed only fourth over 2m7f at Bangor last month but sports first-time cheekpieces today and Paul Nicholls is evidently keen to move her up in trip again and switch her to fences.
runners.comment: Won over hurdles here (2m5f) in April and might improve again over fences

runners.name: Honey I'm Good
runners.spotlight: Four-time chase winner (once over C&D); soundly beaten over hurdles in February, after a long absence, and didn't achieve much when third of four at Fakenham (2m7f, good) in April; reverts to chasing on a good mark but her current ability is hard to gauge.
runners.comment: Reverts to chasing on a good mark but her current ability is hard to gauge

runners.name: Bucks Dream
runners.spotlight: Made the frame in two maiden points last month but faces tougher opposition in this first handicap chase since April 2023; fitted with first-time cheekpieces for stable debut; others look safer.
runners.comment: Made the frame in two maiden points last month but faces tougher opposition here

runners.name: Benny Baloo
runners.spotlight: Well beaten all four hurdle/chase runs since returning from long absence for new stable last September but has slipped to a basement mark and might appreciate getting away from soft/heavy ground; dangerous to ignore.
runners.comment: Has not shown much for current stable but may appreciate getting away from slow ground
